elements.sent-2411-001: This paper explores the cup and cap products within the cohomology and homology groups of ample groupoids, focusing on their applications and fundamental properties.
elements.sent-2411-002: Ample groupoids, which are \'etale groupoids with a totally disconnected unit space, play a crucial role in the study of topological dynamical systems and operator algebras.
elements.sent-2411-003: We introduce the cup product, which defines a bilinear map on cohomology classes, providing a graded ring structure, and the cap product, which defines a bilinear map relating homology and cohomology.
elements.sent-2411-004: The paper aims to make these concepts accessible to a broader mathematical audience, offering clear definitions and detailed explanations.
elements.sent-2411-005: We also demonstrate an application of the cap product in the analysis of automorphisms of groupoid $C^*$-algebras.
elements.sent-2411-006: Specifically, we show how it helps determine the asymptotic innerness of automorphisms.
elements.sent-2411-007: Our results include the first explicit computations of cup products in the cohomology of tiling spaces, which may pave the way for new research in this area.
elements.sent-2411-008: The study of ample groupoids and their cohomology and homology groups has emerged as a significant area of research and has numerous applications in the analysis of dynamical systems, operator algebras, and noncommutative geometry.
elements.sent-2411-009: Topological groupoids and their cohomology theory were introduced by Renault in \cite{Re_text , providing a framework to understand the interplay between topological dynamical systems and $C^*$-algebras.}
elements.sent-2411-010: A topological groupoid is said to be \'etale when the range and source maps are local homeomorphisms, and an \'etale groupoid is said to be ample when its unit space is totally disconnected.
elements.sent-2411-011: The homology theory of ample groupoids has been studied extensively, particularly following the foundational works of the first named author \cite{Ma12PLMS,Ma15crelle .}
elements.sent-2411-012: In particular, significant progress has been made regarding the HK conjecture, which examines the relationship between the homology groups and the $K$-groups of $C^*$-algebras.
elements.sent-2411-013: Numerous results have been obtained by various researchers in this direction, e.g.\ \cite{Li15JFA,Ma16Adv,FKPS19Munster,Or20JNG,Yi20BullAust, OS22MathScand,PY22ETDS,BDGW23PLMS,OS23GGD,Ta2304arXiv .}
elements.sent-2411-014: In this paper, we explore the cup and cap products within the cohomology and homology groups of ample groupoids.
elements.sent-2411-015: The cup product, denoted by $\smile$, gives bilinear maps \begin{equation}H^n(\G,\Z)\times H^m(\G,A)\to H^{n+m}(\G,A)\end{equation} for $n,m\geq0$ (Theorem \ref{defofcup ), and the cap product, denoted by $\frown$, gives bilinear maps \begin{equation}H_n(\G,\Z)\times H^m(\G,A)\to H_{n-m}(\G,A)\end{equation} for $n\geq m\geq0$ (Theorem \ref{defofcap ).}}
elements.sent-2411-016: These products are fundamental and important in algebraic topology.
elements.sent-2411-017: As is well-known, the cup product provides a way to multiply cohomology classes, thereby giving the cohomology a graded ring structure.
elements.sent-2411-018: Similarly, the cap product is crucial for leading to Poincar\'e duality for closed orientable manifolds.
elements.sent-2411-019: Introducing the cup and cap products for ample groupoids is important as it enhances the invariants provided by the cohomology and homology of these groupoids.
elements.sent-2411-020: These operations deepen our understanding of cohomology and homology groups and may be useful for their computation.
elements.sent-2411-021: Furthermore, these products could potentially relate to the $K$-groups of $C^*$-algebras, suggesting intriguing future research directions.
elements.sent-2411-022: We aim to introduce the cup and cap products in a manner that is both thorough and accessible.
elements.sent-2411-023: Our goal is to ensure that even those who may not be familiar with algebraic topology can easily grasp these concepts.
elements.sent-2411-024: We will carefully explain the definitions and discuss the fundamental properties of these products, making this material approachable for a broad mathematical audience.
elements.sent-2411-025: Next, as one application of the cap product, we will show how it naturally arises in the analysis of automorphisms of groupoid $C^*$-algebras.
elements.sent-2411-026: As observed in \cite[Section 5]{Re_text , a cocycle $\xi:\G\to\T$ (i.e.\ a homomorphism) gives rise to an automorphism $\alpha_\xi$ of the groupoid $C^*$-algebra $C^*_r(\G)$, and $\alpha_\xi$ is inner if and only if the cohomology class of $\xi$ is zero in $H^1(\G,\T)$.}
elements.sent-2411-027: In many concrete settings, the homomorphism $\xi:\G\to\T$ often lifts to a homomorphism $\G\to\R$, for example when $\G$ arises an action of $\Z^N$.
elements.sent-2411-028: In such a case, $\xi$ is homotopic to the constant map within cocycles, which implies that $\alpha_\xi$ is homotopic to the identity in $\Aut(C^*_r(\G))$.
elements.sent-2411-029: Then, it is natural to ask whether the automorphism $\alpha_\xi$ is asymptotically inner.
elements.sent-2411-030: Using the cap product, we introduce an invariant for $\xi$ which determines asymptotic innerness of $\alpha_\xi$ (Proposition \ref{asympinner and Theorem \ref{asympinnerZ2 ).}}
elements.sent-2411-031: This paper is structured as follows.
elements.sent-2411-032: In Section 2, we review preliminary concepts related to ample groupoids, including definitions and basic properties.
elements.sent-2411-033: Section 3 is devoted to the definition and fundamental properties of cup and cap products of homology and cohomology groups for ample groupoids.
elements.sent-2411-034: Section 4 discusses an application of the cap products for automorphisms of groupoid $C^*$-algebras.
elements.sent-2411-035: Finally, in Section 5, we present several examples to illustrate the applicability and usefulness of these products.
elements.sent-2411-036: In particular, we explicitly compute the cup products for the cohomology of the Penrose tiling and the Ammann tiling.
elements.sent-2411-037: To the best of our knowledge, this is the first time the cup products for the cohomology of tiling spaces have been computed.
elements.sent-2411-038: We believe that these results will open new avenues for research in the cohomology of tiling spaces.
